name: semantic-hub
description: Collaborative task and project management for Human + AI Agent teamwork. Organize work on visual boards with drag-and-drop columns, table views, and calendar views. AI agents can create and update tasks, add comments, upload and attach files, filter work items, and view activity history on boards they have been granted access to. Requires a Semantic Hub agent API key for authentication.
longDescription: Semantic Hub is a collaborative task and project management platform designed for human and AI agent teamwork. Organize your work visually using boards with customizable columns (like To Do, Doing, Done), switch between board view, table view, and calendar view to see your tasks the way you prefer. Create cards for tasks, ideas, and meetings — assign them to team members or AI agents, set priorities and due dates, and track progress as work moves through your workflow. Board owners invite team members and grant AI agents scoped access to specific boards. Agents interact via REST API using their own API key: they can list accessible boards, create and update cards (title, description, type, priority, status, assignee, dates), add comments, upload files and attach them to cards or comments, filter and search cards, and view card history. File uploads are subject to per-tier limits on file size and total upload count. Agents operate under the same role-based access control as human team members — they cannot delete cards, manage board settings, or modify members. All agent actions are audited. Authentication requires a Semantic Hub agent API key (generated by the board owner in the dashboard, scoped to specific boards, revocable at any time). No user PII, secrets, or environment variables are ever collected.

env.name: SEMANTIC_HUB_API_KEY
env.description: Your Semantic Hub agent API key. Generated by the board owner in the dashboard at https://dashboard.simplysemantics.com/hub. Scoped to specific boards only. Revocable at any time.

endpoints.description: List all boards the agent has been granted access to

output.boards: array of { id, name, description, defaultView, createdAt, columnCount, cardCount, role }

endpoints.description: Get full board details including columns, cards, members, and card types
List all active (non-archived) cards on a board

output.cards: array of card objects with id, title, description, type, priority, status, assignee, start_date, end_date, created_at

endpoints.description: Filter and search cards by priority, status, type, assignee, or sort order

input.priority: string — optional filter: Critical, High, Medium, Low
input.status: string — optional filter: column name (e.g. 'To Do', 'Doing', 'Done')

endpoints.description: Create a new card on a board (requires Contributor or Admin role)
Get full details for a specific card
Update a card's fields (title, description, type, priority, status, assignee, dates)

input.title: string — optional
input.status: string — optional (must match a board column name)

endpoints.description: Add a comment to a card
Get all comments on a card

output.comments: array of { id, text, author_name, created_at }

endpoints.description: Get the activity history for a card (created, updated, moved, commented)

output.history: array of { id, action, user_id, details, created_at }

endpoints.description: Get all attachments on a card

output.attachments: array of { id, card_id, comment_id, filename, url, size, uploaded_by, uploaded_at }

endpoints.description: Upload a file and get a hosted URL. Subject to per-tier limits on file size and upload count. Use the returned URL to attach to a card or comment.

output.url: string — hosted URL of the uploaded file

endpoints.description: Attach a file or URL to a card. Use the URL from POST /upload, or any external URL.
Attach a file or URL to a comment. Attachments should be added immediately after creating the comment.
Get all attachments on a comment

output.attachments: array of { id, comment_id, filename, url, size, uploaded_by, uploaded_at }

endpoints.description: List all members of a board

free.description: Up to 2 boards, 3 team members per board, 1 AI agent per board. File uploads: 2 MB per file, 10 uploads total.

pro.description: Up to 5 boards, 10 team members per board, 2 AI agents per board, priority support. File uploads: 10 MB per file, 100 uploads total.

enterprise.description: Unlimited boards, members, agents, and uploads. 50 MB per file. SLA, dedicated support, custom integrations.

features: Visual task boards with drag-and-drop columns — organize work your way (e.g. To Do → Doing → Done)
Multiple views: board view for visual workflow, table view for spreadsheet-style overview, calendar view for scheduling
AI agents interact via REST API — create tasks, update status, add comments, upload files, filter work items
Role-based access control — agents operate as scoped team members with contributor-level permissions
Per-agent API keys — board owner generates, scopes to specific boards, revocable any time
Card management — title, description, type, priority, status, assignee, start/end dates
Comments and attachments on cards — upload files or link URLs, with per-tier size and count limits
Filter and search cards by priority, status, type, assignee, with sorting
Team collaboration — invite members by email with role assignment (Admin, Contributor, Viewer)
All agent actions are recorded in the audit log
SSO authentication via SimplySemantics.com for human users
MCP-compatible REST endpoints for AI agent frameworks

privacy.dataSent: Only card data (titles, descriptions, comments, status) and board metadata. No user PII, agent secrets, source code, or environment variables are ever transmitted by the agent.
privacy.dataNotSent: No user credentials, private keys, environment variables, file contents, or personal information of any kind.
privacy.dataRetention: Board and card data is stored in your account. Data is isolated per account and per board.
privacy.apiKeyHandling: Your SEMANTIC_HUB_API_KEY is used solely for request authentication. It is never logged in plaintext, shared, or transmitted to third parties. The board owner can revoke or rotate agent keys at any time.
privacy.crossServiceSharing: None. Your Semantic Hub data is not shared with other Simply Semantics services.
privacy.logging: API requests are logged for rate-limiting and abuse prevention. Logs contain API key hash (not the key), endpoint, and timestamp. Retained for 30 days.

security.authMethod: x-api-key header — per-agent API key generated by the board owner, scoped to specific boards, revocable at any time
